mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 3.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal, with lower rates directly supporting higher forward equity valuations and returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 3.5% do not exceed baseline by at least 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration and recession risk. This threshold-triggered condition activates a negative signal for S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice equity risk premiums upward and reduce growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly decline by > 5% month-over-month, indicating synchronized demand destruction across consumer and residential sectors. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns as forward earnings guidance deteriorates and equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 5% MoM simultaneously
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint decline signal has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 6-month trend by > 1.5%, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 5-day returns as markets reprrice growth expectations upward and corporate profit margins expand.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns during INDPRO acceleration periods do not exceed baseline by at least 1% annualized (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k net adds per month, triggering a threshold-based signal of labor market softening. This condition predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns as investors anticipate Fed policy reversal and lower corporate earnings growth from reduced consumer spending capacity.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth falls below 100k adds
regime_hint: labor market deceleration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 3.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.0%, creating a negative real rate regime. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns as real rate compression incentivizes equity allocation over cash and bonds, supporting valuation multiples.
regime_hint: negative real rate environment with financial repression
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle or recessionary conditions. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 60-day returns as equity valuations compress and earnings revisions turn negative in response to growth deceleration.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of low-growth periods, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 2200k units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) grow > 0.5% MoM, indicating synchronized demand strength across residential and consumer sectors. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 5-day returns as forward earnings expectations rise and risk premiums compress.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 2200k annualized AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 58% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
